sections.title: Admission Requirements
sections.content: To be considered for admission into the first year of the Bachelor's degree programme, candidates should have passed five subjects relevant to the programme at credit level at not more than two sittings in the General Certificate of Education (GCE) Ordinary Level Examination, West African School Certificate Examination (WASCE), Senior Secondary School Certificate Examination organized by the West African Examination Council (WAEC), National Examinations Council (NECO), National Business and Technical Examination Board (NABTEB) or their equivalents. Furthermore, candidates must obtain an acceptable score in the Unified Tertiary Matriculation Examination (UTME) and further obtain an acceptable score at the University screening test where applicable. In addition, School and Departmental subject admission requirements must be satisfied. Credit passes in English Language and Mathematics are required of all University degree candidates.

sections.title: Deferment
sections.content:
A fresh student may apply to Senate through the Registrar for deferment of admission not later than one month after registration. To qualify for deferment a student must be fully registered. Application for the deferment of admission is subject to the approval of Senate. Deferment shall be valid for only one academic year.

Applications for deferment which are made after the stipulated period shall not be honoured.

sections.title: Voluntary Suspension of Studies
sections.content:
A duly registered student may for good cause (ill-health, financial difficulty) apply for voluntary suspension of studies from the University by completing and submitting to his/her Department a Voluntary Suspension of Studies Form collected from the Academic Affairs Division. Permission for Voluntary Suspension of Studies shall be granted only by Senate. Voluntary Suspension shall last for one (1) Academic Session only.

Where an affected student fails to apply for Voluntary Suspension of Studies, he/she will be automatically withdrawn after one (1) semester.

sections.title: Re-admission
sections.content:
A student who wishes to be re-admitted after a period of Voluntary Suspension of Studies shall apply two months before the beginning of the Session by completing and submitting the Re-admission Form in the Academic Affairs Division.

A student who fails to return at the end of the Voluntary Suspension of Studies shall automatically be withdrawn.

sections.title: Change of Programme of Study
sections.intro: Any student who desires to change his/her programme of study may be permitted to do so under the following conditions:
list: i. A vacancy exists in the course of study which he/she seeks a change;
ii. He/she must possess the minimum entry requirements for Admission into the desired course of study at the time the change is sought;
iii. Such change of course of study shall be subject to approval by the Senate;
iv. He/she must have spent a minimum of one (1) Academic year in the course of Admission.
v. He/she must have gotten a minimum 3.0 CGPA at the end of the session prior to the sought of change of programme

sections.title: Student Class Attendance
sections.content:
Attendance at lectures, tutorials, studio, laboratory, workshop and practical sessions, etc is compulsory and shall attract 5% of the total continuous assessment score.
Every student is required to meet a minimum of 75% class attendance in order to qualify for the examination in a given course.

sections.title: Eligibility
sections.content: A student admitted into the University is eligible to register for the specific programme for which the candidate was offered admission if he/she possesses the necessary requirements.

sections.title: Academic Advisers
sections.content: Students will be assigned to Academic Advisers who shall acquaint them with academic matters and regulations.

sections.title: Counseling
sections.content: The Student Affairs Division will have a team of Counsellors. All these Counsellors working under the University Counsellor will provide counselling services to students.

sections.title: Enrolment
sections.content:
During registration at the beginning of each Session, all students shall enroll in person in all courses for which they are eligible by signing their names on the official course register for each course.

Students should consult their School timetable for the schedule of lectures/tutorials/practicals for all courses registered in their respective programmes.

sections.title: Definition of a Credit Unit
sections.content: A credit unit is defined as one hour of lecture, or one hour of tutorial, or three hours of practicals (workshop, laboratory, or field work) per week per semester.

sections.title: Student Academic Workload
sections.content: All full-time students shall take a minimum of 15 credit units and a maximum of 24 credit units per semester.

sections.title: Add and Drop a Course
sections.intro: A student can add or drop a course in any particular semester if
list: i. He/she is no longer interested in the course earlier registered for provided that such a course is an elective course.
ii. He/she registers below the minimum number of credit unit or above the maximum number of credit units.
iii. He/she has unregistered carry over course(s). In this case, even a core course can be dropped in order to make room for carry over courses to be registered first.
iv. A student should also be aware of pre-requisite courses in his/her programme and should first register for failed lower courses.
sections.additional_info:
A student adding a course should do so not more than two (2) weeks after the commencement of semester lectures.
In all cases of additional courses, the student must ensure that 75% attendance at lectures is satisfied.

sections.title: Concurrent Enrolment
sections.content: Students are not permitted to enroll in programmes concurrently either within the University, including University's evening programmes or in other Educational Institutions without the approval of Senate.

sections.title: Duration of Semester
sections.content: The duration of the semester is eighteen (18) weeks. Fifteen (15) weeks shall normally be reserved for teaching, while three (3) weeks shall be reserved for examinations.

sections.title: Continuous Assessment
sections.content: Assessment of students' work shall be continuous and this shall form not more than 30% of the total assessment for the course. Every student is advised to partake in continuous assessment. Any student who fails to participate in continuous assessment shall score a grade of F in that course irrespective of his/her performance in the examination.

sections.title: Final Examination
sections.content: There shall be a Final Examination at the end of the semester. The final examination shall form not less than 70% of the course grade.

sections.title: Grading System
sections.content: A student's academic work shall be graded at the end of every semester using the following letter grades:

sections.title: Appeals for Reassessment of Examination Scripts
sections.note: Numbering duplications present in source text (e.g. 3.15, 3.16, 3.17 appear twice).
sections.content: A student may request for a reassessment of the quality of his/her work in a course examination during the semester and within two weeks after publication of results. When a student makes such a request, he/she shall pay a reassessment charge. The application for reassessment shall begin only after presentation of evidence of payment. The charge is refundable only if the appeal is successful. The reassessment shall be final when approved by Senate.

sections.title: Incomplete Grade
sections.content: An incomplete grade can be obtained if either the Continuous Assessment score or examination score is not available. If an incomplete grade is not remedied at the next examination for a course, the student is deemed to have failed the examination.

sections.title: Absence from an Examination
sections.content:
A student, who absents himself/herself from an examination without a written permission from the Head of Department or Provost of School during or at the end of the semester, will be deemed to have failed the examination. A student who has not met 75% attendance and has been disqualified from taking the examination will be deemed to have failed the examination.

If a student is absent from an examination on permission, he will be allowed to write the examination at the next available opportunity. The units of the course(s) shall not be used to compute the student's GPA in the semester of deferment.

sections.title: Grade Point (GP)
sections.content:
For the purpose of computing academic standing status of a student at the end of the semester, Grade Points per unit are assigned as follows: A=5, B=4, C=3, D=2, E=1 and F=0.

Units of courses in which a grade of 'I' (Incomplete) is earned are excluded from grade point computation for the semester in which the 'I' is earned. Upon removal of grade 'I' of if 'I' changes into an 'F' or other grades, the units are included in the subsequent cumulative grade point averages.

sections.title: Grade Point Average (GPA)
sections.content:
Grade Point Average is obtained by multiplying the Grade Point attained in each course by the number of Credit Units assigned to that course and then summing these up for all the courses and dividing by the total number of Credit Units taken in the semester.

To complete the Calculative Grade Point Average, the total Grade Points multiplied by the respective Credits Units for all the semesters are added and then divided by the total number of Credit Units for all courses registered by the student.

sections.title: Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA)
sections.content:
Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) is the sum of all the points earned in all the semesters divided by the total number of Credit Units attempted in all the semesters. CGPA calculation begins in the second semester of 100 level and continues thereafter till the end of the programme.
The formula is as follows:
CGPA = CTGP / CTCR

Where CTGP is the Cumulative Total Grade Points
CTCR is the Cumulative Total Credits Registered

sections.title: Repeat Grade
sections.note: This section is numbered 3.15 in the source document, despite previous sections with higher numbers.
sections.content: When a student re-registers for and actually repeats a course and takes the examination in the course, he/she should be credited with the actual grade scored in the repeated examination. The new grade earned in a repeat course does not replace the 'F' grade in the student's record. The result of all the attempts made by the students shall be used in calculating the Final CGPA.

sections.title: Clear Standing or (Good Standing)
sections.content: For a student to be on Clear Academic Standing, he/she should have at least an E grade in all attempted courses in the University.

sections.title: Carry-Over of Courses
sections.content: Where a student fails a course(s) in any semester, the failed course(s) should be carried over to the next appropriate semester and be registered first before registering for fresh courses for the semester.

sections.title: Academic Probation
sections.content:
A student shall be placed on Academic Probation if he/she fails to maintain a minimum CGPA of 1.00 at the end of the session. The probationary status of a student shall be reversed if the student maintains a CGPA of at least 1.00 in any subsequent academic session. During the period of probation, the student should not register for more than 15 credit units. He/she should begin with the registration of failed courses.

The responsibility to reverse the probationary status rests with the student. A preliminary notice of poor academic standing shall be given to a student in writing by the University.

sections.title: Withdrawal for Academic Failure (WAF)
sections.content:
A student shall be required to withdraw for academic failure if he/she at the end of any probation year fails to maintain a CGPA of at least 1.00.

However, in order to minimize waste of human resources, consideration should be given to withdrawal from programme of study and possible transfer to other programmes within the University provided the student meets the admission requirement of the new programme.
A student shall be allowed to have a maximum of two and half (2½) Sessions for a five (5) year Programme.

sections.title: Inter/Intra – School Transfer
sections.content: A student seeking to transfer from one School to another or one Department to another shall have a minimum CGPA of 3.00. A student seeking to transfer shall complete the University Transfer Form and submit same to the Head of Department from which the student is seeking transfer. All applications for transfer are subject to recommendation of the Faculty Board to which the student is seeking transfer. Approval of such transfers shall be obtained from Senate. He/She must also meet the minimum entry requirements of the programme sought.
sections.note: No student shall be accepted on inter/intra faculty transfer into a level above Year III for a five (5) year or six (6) year programme.

sections.title: Transfer From other Institutions
sections.content:
Students currently registered in any full-time programme in other Universities may transfer to Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi at the beginning of a new session, provided that they have a CGPA of 3.00 in programmes being offered at the Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi. Inter-University Transfer can only be allowed into the same programme that the student had registered in the previous University. He/She must have scored a minimum JAMB cut-off mark approved by FUTIA for 100 Level students for the programme at that time when he/she started 100 Level.

Forms shall be used together with transcripts, in applying through the Registrar. Admission shall be considered by Senate based upon the Registrar's recommendation at least two (2) months before the commencement of the session. There shall also be an attestation by the Registrar of the student's first University that the applicant was not expelled for misconduct from that University.

sections.title: Transfer of Credit Units from other Institutions
sections.content: Students transferring from other recognized Universities may be credited with units for courses successfully completed and which satisfy the course requirements of the programme for which the student is registered at the Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi. The credit units and grade points awarded shall be placed in the appropriate level in the degree programme. A transfer student shall not normally be credited with less than 65 units for courses taken in other Universities for those transferring at end of year II and 95 units for those transferring at the end of year III.

sections.title: Publication of Results
sections.content: The Academic Affairs Division shall publish the results of all examinations after approval by Senate, except otherwise directed by Senate. However, School Boards may publish failed courses of students after Senate's approval. Senate's approved student results could be made available to parents/guardians at the end of every semester on a formal request through the Head of Department and Provost of School to the Registrar.

sections.title: Attendance Registers
sections.content: All registered students of the University are expected to attend all scheduled classes, including tutorials, laboratory work, and field trips. Student attendance at scheduled class/tutorial shall be taken at every class/tutorial session and counter-signed by the lecturer in-charge. Attendance Registers for every course will be cross-checked by the Head of Department from time-to-time. Attendance requirements to sit for course examinations shall be a minimum of 75% of scheduled classes.

sections.title: Duration of Programme
sections.content:
A student in a five-year degree programme shall normally be expected to complete the programme in ten (10) semesters but may be allowed to continue for a total of (15) semesters and still qualify for a classified degree provided he/she maintains a CGPA of 1.00 and above.
However, a student who is unable to complete the programme in fifteen (15) semesters may be allowed to continue for an additional two (2) semesters but shall only be eligible for a pass degree, irrespective of the student's CGPA.

In general, the maximum number of extra sessions shall be fifty percent (50%) of the programme duration.

sections.title: Graduation Requirements for Bachelor's Degrees
sections.intro: In order to qualify for the Bachelor's Degree of this University, students must attain:
list: i. A pass grade in supervised Student Industrial Work Experience Scheme, (SIWES) where applicable;
iii. A minimum of 150 units including SIWES for a five–year programme. A transfer student must earn a minimum of 90 units for a 5-year programme in Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi;
iv. Pass the prescribed general studies courses;
v. Pass all compulsory courses in the degree programme in which he/she is registered;

vi. Any student who has stayed in the University for more than one year in excess of the normal length of the degree programme for academic reasons, may only be awarded a 'Pass' degree (for example, seven (7) years for a five (5) year programme).

sections.title: Final Classification of Degree
sections.content: For the purpose of final classification of degrees, a student should have achieved the following CGPA at the end of 150 or more units for a 5-year programme.

sections.title: RULES FOR THE CONDUCT OF UNIVERSITY EXAMINATION
sections.note: This section number duplicates a previous section number (3.25 Clear Standing) found on page 17, but appears on page 21 in the source text.
rules_list: 1. It is the responsibility of each student intending to take any University Examination to ascertain the date, time and venue of the particular examination as indicated in the examination timetable.
i. Candidates must present themselves at the examination venue thirty (30) minutes to the scheduled time of commencement of the examination with evidence of payment of fees.

ii. Candidates must not be admitted into the Examination Hall after the examination has been in progress for thirty (30) minutes. Candidates will not also be allowed to leave the Examination Hall during the first thirty minutes and the last fifteen (15) minutes of an examination.

iii. Candidates will not be permitted to start an examination until formally instructed to do so by the Examiner/Invigilator in-charge of the examination. Candidates wishing to leave the Examination Hall must obtain the express permission of the Examiner/Invigilator before doing so.

iv. Any candidate permitted to leave an Examination Hall temporarily must hand over his/her question paper(s) and answer script(s) to the Examiner/invigilator and must be accompanied by the Examiner/Invigilator or a person appointed by the Examiner/Invigilator. Female students can only be accompanied by female invigilators or officers. Male students can only be accompanied by male invigilators or officers.

2. Candidates can only take printed materials or manuscript into an Examination Hall if it has been previously announced in the examination timetable and if it is stated in the instructions to the question paper that such material may be used.

3. All bags, brief cases and other materials not permitted for use in the examination must be deposited at a designated place. Candidates will, however, be allowed to use their own mathematical instruments/tables and such other aids as the Examiner/Invigilator may permit. No such materials are allowed into examination halls.

4. The use of mobile phones, wristwatches, programmable calculators, information storage devices, instructional manuals, textbooks, atlases, lecture notes, etc shall not be allowed in the Examination Halls. Implant of any electronic devices in the body is an examination offence.
5. Candidates must sit at the desk assigned to them by the invigilator. The University Administration will not condone any swapping of seats by candidates.
6. It is compulsory for candidates to sign the Examination Attendance Register while the examination is in progress and sign out the same register at the end of every examination.
7. Candidates must ensure that they sign out the attendance register as they hand over their answer script to the examiner/invigilator before they leave the examination hall.
8. Smoking, chewing of gum and the consumption of food and drinks are not permitted during the Examination.
9. All questions in written Examination must be answered in simple English unless instructions on the question paper indicate otherwise.

10. It is an academic offence punishable by expulsion from the University for any candidate(s) to prevent other candidate(s) from taking the scheduled University examinations or cause obstruction to examination processes.

sections.title: PROCEDURE FOR HANDLING SUSPECTED EXAMINATION MISCONDUCT CASES
sections.intro: The following procedures shall apply in handling all suspected cases of examination misconduct in the University.
procedures_list: 1. The attention of any other invigilator present should be drawn to the suspicious circumstance(s), if an Invigilator suspects that a candidate has committed examination misconduct. If another student suspects that there is misconduct, he/she should alert the Invigilator(s)
2. The invigilator(s) should approach the suspected candidate and inform him/her of the suspicion, and give the candidate an examination misconduct form to fill as evidence.

3. The Invigilator(s) should withdraw the candidate's script and issue a fresh script for him/her to continue with the examination. If any unauthorized material is discovered it should be confiscated by the Invigilator(s). The withdrawn script, the new script and other unauthorized materials should be tendered as evidence against the candidate.
4. Within twenty-four hours, the Invigilator(s) should make a written report to the Chief Examiner, who is the Head of the Department.

5. Written accounts of the incident by the Invigilator(s) along with the suspect's script and statement plus any unauthorized materials confiscated by the Invigilator(s), the Chief Examiner's comment, and other candidates (if appropriate in evidence) must be submitted under confidential cover to the Provost of the School who shall forward it to the Senate Examination Misconduct Committee within twenty-four (24) hours at the end of the course examination.

If for any reason a candidate suspected to be involved in examination malpractice refuses to sign or cooperate with the invigilator, the invigilator shall not engage in any physical fight but completes the relevant sections of the examination malpractice form and forward accordingly.

6. However, if during the marking, moderating or collating of examination scripts or results, an Examiner or any member of staff suspects that misconduct has taken place, the Examiner or member of staff must confer with the Chief Examiner (Head of Department). As soon as a prima facie case has been established, the Examiner or member of staff must submit written reports with the student's script and other corroborating evidence to the Provost of the School through the Chief Examiner (Head of Department) within seventy-two (72) hours. The student so affected must be informed immediately by the Head of Department and made to submit a written statement.
7. The suspect will be invited to appear before, and be heard by the Senate Examination Misconduct Committee.
8. The decision of the Senate Examination Misconduct Committee shall be conveyed to the candidate within seventy-two (72) hours.
9. All cases of examination misconduct must be disposed of within the shortest possible time but not longer than six (6) weeks.
10. All materials confiscated from students in proven cases of misconduct shall be kept by the University until punishment has been served.

subsections.title: Matriculation
subsections.content: The University has, as one of its programmes of activities, the matriculation of students. During matriculation, only duly registered students shall be formally admitted as members of the University with a pledge of loyalty to the ideals of the University.

subsections.title: Convocation
subsections.content: The University shall also have convocation for the conferment of degrees, diplomas, certificates, awards and honours as approved by Senate.
